content: The recursive self-referential pattern of repeated "agent" labeling in Topic B acts as a built-in curiosity signal that can train the episodic memory of DeepResearch Agents, where the repeated uncontextualized mentions of "agent" become low-density prediction errors that drive the agent to actively seek out missing contextual information to resolve the uncertainty. This turns unstructured incomplete search results into a free intrinsic reward signal for active memory expansion.
